title: Add --no-creds flag to skopeo inspect
body:
Implements https://github.com/projectatomic/skopeo/issues/421

Currently skopeo inspect allows to:
* Use the default credentials in $HOME/.docker.config
* Explicitly define credentials via de --creds flag

This implements a --no-creds flag which will query docker registries anonymously.

comments.body: Hi, the travis build failure looks unrelated to my changes, can it be relaunched?

The changes LGTM.  Not sure what's going on with Travis.  I concur, doesn't look like an issue with your submit, but rather some Ruby versioning issues.  @runcom do you know what's up with that or someone we can talk to for it?
That failure is likely unrelated, it was not entirely obvious so from a quick look whether this is something Travis can fix for us or whether we need to update our build scripts.

https://github.com/kubernetes-incubator/cri-o/pull/1015 eventually suggests there should be a way to override the default config paths, and use something else; that might indirectly help here (`--auth-file /dev/null` or so), but I’m still curious when would a user want to not use the credentials required to make things work.

The scenario I've encountered and why I have implemented it is the following:

I'm an openshift / atomic-registry administrator. A user complains about not being able to pull an image that should be available for everyone. I want to test it as system:unauthenticated. As I'm authenticated as the cluster admin here I have to choices:
1- `docker logout && skopeo inspect docker://...`
2- `HOME=/non/existing skopeo inspect docker://...`

Besides I use atomic-registry (the version shipped in OCP). If you try to access atomic-registry with no credentials you'll be considered `system:anonymous` but if you try to access it with an expired token you'll get an access denied. In this scenario, for automated things it's very handy for me to be able to run it with `--no-creds` so that I don't have to worry about having an expired token.

My main use for skopeo is debugging registries and I think many people will end up using it the same way

I added the flag --no-creds for delete (which is working) and for copy --src-no-creds and --dest-no-creds. The copy flags aren't working but  apparently the --src-tls-verify and --dest-tls-verify aren't either. I'll investigate this as a separate issue as soon as this PR is accepted or denied (to avoid merging conflicts)

Ruby is making Travis unhappy.  I don't believe it's created by these changes.
"Homebrew must be run under Ruby 2.3! (RuntimeError)"

@TomSweeneyRedHat I've tested manually and I'm not certain but I think it isn't working.

I'll test it in a serious manner but I'm doing this on my free time so it's gonna be on hold until the weekend.

>
> I'm an openshift / atomic-registry administrator. A user complains about not being able to pull an image that should be available for everyone. I want to test it as system:unauthenticated. As I'm authenticated as the cluster admin here I have to choices:

I'll investigate this as a separate issue as soon as this PR is accepted or denied (to avoid merging conflicts)

FWIW `skopeo copy --src-creds` does work for me.
